meaning: A chatty, silly person who can't stop talking about nothing.
A sneaky, untrustworthy person who smiles too much.
Going in the wrong direction, usually counterclockwise and probably into a wall.
Complete and utter nonsense said with total confidence.
To confuse someone so thoroughly they forget what they were doing.
To run away very quickly, usually from something embarrassing.
A loud commotion caused by something completely avoidable.
To wander around slowly doing absolutely nothing productive.
Completely crooked, skewed, or just badly positioned.
Absolute rubbish. Often spoken loudly at family dinners.
Nonsense spoken by someone who believes they are very clever.
A young person who is far too confident for someone their age.
British nonsense of the highest order. Usually said by grandparents.
Absolute nonsense delivered with unnecessary drama.
A person of spectacularly poor judgment.
A large knife, or the act of fighting with large knives very dramatically.
To completely baffle someone to the point of visible confusion.
To thoroughly confuse and unsettle someone's brain.
A loud and chaotic fuss about something that will be forgotten tomorrow.
To trick or deceive someone, usually with a very straight face.
Complete nonsense, slightly worse than balderdash.
Nonsense. Slightly fancier than malarkey.
A small lie told with enormous theatrical commitment.
A long, pointless, and confusing process nobody asked for.
Someone who insists on cuddling everything, including strangers.
An umbrella used with far more drama than necessary.
To cuddle and whisper in a way that annoys everyone nearby.
To waste time in the most charming and infuriating way possible.
A tiny, inconsequential wobble that somehow breaks everything.
Fast, confident speech that means absolutely nothing.
An informal party where everyone insists on making noise.
A word used when you completely forget the name of the thing.
Any object whose name you can't remember or never learned.
A small part of something that you poke or wiggle to fix it.
A disorganised, noisy commotion, usually involving children.
A dramatic uproar over something that didn't warrant it.
Nervous butterflies in the stomach, but worse and wobblier.
A suppressed, poorly hidden laugh at someone else's expense.
So infatuated you forget how to function as a normal person.
The satisfying sound of something large hitting water.
To speak at great length about things you only vaguely understand.
To dress up extremely flashily for a very mundane occasion.
Official-sounding language designed to confuse and impress.
A minor collision between two very embarrassed people.
A person who talks an enormous amount of confident nonsense.
Impressively bold in a way that makes others uncomfortable.
The loud, embarrassing rumbling noise your stomach makes in meetings.
A noisy quarrel that began over something absolutely trivial.
So confused you're standing with your mouth slightly open.
Having a remarkably well-shaped bottom. It's a real word.
A large, angry lump that appears at the worst possible time.
Positioned in a completely chaotic, skewed direction.
Trickery performed with great style and questionable ethics.
Showy, empty talk designed to get applause from gullible people.
A person who barges into every door they encounter.
A clumsy, dim-witted individual with surprising confidence.
A small person with a wildly inflated sense of self-importance.
An old person who gives unrequested opinions on everything.
Feeling extremely unwell after eating or drinking far too much.
A grumpy person who finds fault with literally everything.
To throw something, or someone, out of a window with purpose.
A loveable but spectacularly silly person.
A complete idiot who doesn't know they're a complete idiot.
An old word for bagpipes. The name suits them perfectly.
A person so dense that good ideas just bounce right off them.
Spending a lot of time doing nothing useful while looking busy.
A state of extreme fidgety anxiety caused by nothing specific.
A silly, flirty person or a whirring spinning toy.
Total nonsense wrapped in confident, enthusiastic packaging.
A dialect of gibberish spoken exclusively by very excited people.
Someone who is flashy and a bit too eager to be noticed.
Complete rubbish of the absolute finest quality.
A big fuss made over something completely insignificant.
A foolish, insignificant person who takes themselves very seriously.
To pretend to work while doing absolutely nothing of substance.
A person who worries excessively about small, unimportant things.
To wander around enjoying yourself when you should be responsible.
Someone with a memorably odd and punchable-looking face.
A dizzy, scatterbrained person who loses their train of thought mid-sen...
A gullible person who believes every single thing they are told.
Bizarre, extreme, and completely detached from conventional reality.
A well-meaning person who manages to mess up the simplest tasks.
A British exclamation of shock said when something is ridiculous.
A grumpy person who mutters complaints under their breath constantly.
Miscellaneous junk or an informal term for a foolish person.
A seller of small sewing items with an unnecessarily fancy title.
Someone operating at approximately 50% of expected intelligence.
Hopelessly unlucky in a way that is almost impressive.
In a completely disorganised, chaotic jumble.
An awkward, gangly young person who hasn't grown into themselves yet.
A random, messy mixture of things thrown together without thought.
Acting superior and snobbish for absolutely no good reason.
To cheat or trick someone with impressive levels of audacity.
A noisy confusion of voices, usually in a crowded small room.
The practice of deliberate deception carried out with great flair.
A cheeky, impudent person who needs to be taken down a peg.
Sneaky, dishonest behaviour disguised as completely normal activity.
Someone who falls over things that weren't even in their way.
A person whose best ideas are consistently terrible.
To move in a bouncy, floppy, deeply undignified manner.
A lazy, lethargic person who moves like they're underwater.
A big, clumsy person who apologises after every collision.
A magpie, but called this when it has stolen something important.
Talking in a rambling, confused way that goes absolutely nowhere.
Being indirect and overly cautious when you just need to say the thing.
A person who is timid and weak in every imaginable situation.
A foolish daydreamer who trips over things while staring at clouds.
Someone who sits on the fence and refuses to commit to any opinion.
Stubbornly holding onto a wrong belief even after being corrected.
Weak, overly sentimental, and completely lacking in decisiveness.
A particularly advanced level of nincompoop.
Someone whose brain is present but not entirely participating.
To poke around aimlessly, or an affectionate word for a silly head.
A person whose skull appears to contain very little of consequence.
A large, clumsy person with very little social awareness.
Any object you can't name but need urgently.
A long, complicated, unnecessary conversation about a simple thing.
A foolish old man or a very strange pair of trousers.
The wiggling of toes when someone is nervous or excited.
Excessively fussy about completely unimportant details.
The practice of arguing about tiny points while missing the big ones.
Nonsense of a light, fluffy, completely harmless variety.
Trivially small and utterly beneath anyone's attention.
A British word for a complete and utter foolish person.
An insignificant, unimpressive person with an outsized opinion.
A British term for someone who does something spectacularly daft.
A complete and utter coward of the most dramatic variety.
Self-important in a way that everyone around you finds exhausting.
To quibble over minor details in the most annoying way possible.
A fall directly onto your bottom, usually in front of people.
Playfully mischievous in a way that's hard to stay mad about.
Eager to argue or fight over absolutely anything at all.
A person who sells fake cures with enormous enthusiasm.
To argue about something so small it barely exists.
A scruffy, dishevelled person who looks charming despite it.
Uncontrollably boisterous in an exhausting but endearing way.
A mischievous rogue who you can't help but like a little.
Disturbingly loud and disorderly in a joyful way.
Showy, exciting activity designed to dazzle and distract.
People considered undesirable by someone who also probably is.
A long, drawn out, utterly pointless procedure.
Wildly exciting and probably slightly dangerous.
Low-quality drink that probably shouldn't be consumed.
Someone who stares at accidents while pretending they aren't.
A noisy, chaotic disturbance caused by too many opinions.
A rough, rowdy person who enjoys a bit of casual mayhem.
A mischievous rascal who always has a suspicious smile.
A charming troublemaker who never quite learns their lesson.
A person whose thoughts fly in seventeen directions simultaneously.
Someone who casually ignores rules they find personally inconvenient.
A dishonest person who somehow remains likeable.
An eccentric person whose ideas arrive from unexpected angles.
Untidy in a way that suggests a complete lack of mirrors at home.
A single unit of mischievous or silly behaviour.
A loveable fool who is silly in an entirely harmless way.
Sneaky, underhanded behaviour carried out behind closed doors.
Someone who avoids work with an almost professional dedication.
Done quickly and carelessly with zero attention to detail.
Comedy involving falling over, being hit, and general chaos.
Carelessly untidy in a way that implies it is very much a choice.
Done in a messy, careless way with no pride whatsoever.
Messy, careless, and probably a little damp.
Extremely lazy in a way that requires actual effort to achieve.
Someone who stays in bed long after any reasonable hour.
A person who finds fault with everything and complains constantly.
A person who creeps around doing things they shouldn't.
Someone who thinks they're better than you for reasons that aren't real.
Behaving in a manner suggesting superiority that nobody else agrees with.
To nap in a particularly contented, curled-up manner.
To breathe noisily through a blocked nose, usually in a meeting.
Not very bright, but usually very pleasant about it.
British slang for excellent. Mostly used by people over 80.
Someone who borrows things and mysteriously never returns them.
Accidentally swapping sounds in words, like 'blushing crow' for 'crushing blow'.
The gloriously disgusting sound of stepping in something wet.
Slightly drunk, slightly skewed, or slightly confused.
Acting nervous and erratic in a way that makes others uneasy.
Someone who trips, bumbles, and stumbles through every situation.
Behaving as though everyone around you is slightly beneath you.
A person who flatters others constantly for personal benefit.
A sticky sweet, or a crafty flatterer who can't be trusted.
A small, harmless lie told with a completely straight face.
A person dressed in tattered, ragged clothing.
A game of flicking small discs, or slang for something trivial.
Silly, playful behaviour that entertains some and annoys others.
To play a wind instrument badly, or to wander about aimlessly.
Silly, trivial talk or writing of the most pointless variety.
A small, annoying, and rather insignificant silly person.
A foolish, inept person who means well but achieves little.
A state of noisy confusion where nobody agrees on anything.
A wandering person with no fixed home and plenty of stories.
Excessively proud of yourself for reasons nobody else can identify.
Offering nothing of interest or substance whatsoever.
To talk at length without actually saying anything meaningful.
A British term for a foolish, clumsy, or inept person.
Someone sneaky who slides around the edges of every situation.
Someone who gets what they want through flattery and pleading.
A person who constantly changes their opinion to match the room.
To move in a light, uncertain, fluttery way like a lost leaf.
To move back and forth in short, jerky, undignified movements.
A person who talks endlessly without ever reaching a point.
Lacking decisiveness or strength of character in every situation.
Someone who thinks they're funny but very much isn't.
To move unsteadily, usually right before falling over completely.
A person who worries excessively about things that won't happen.
Talking loudly and rapidly about absolutely nothing important.
Someone who talks constantly and doesn't notice your glazed eyes.
Rude and rowdy in a way that suggests no upbringing whatsoever.
Amusingly unusual in a slightly unhinged but harmless way.
Moving in an alternating diagonal pattern, usually to avoid something.
An old wide-mouthed gun, or a clumsy blundering person.
A nerdy expert who knows far too much about one specific thing.
A project that wastes time and money while appearing important.
A stupid, incompetent person with unearned self-confidence.
To move clumsily and confusedly through a situation.
Nonsense, or to escape from somewhere hurriedly.
Talking at length and at speed without any discernible meaning.
Completely broken, ruined, or gone entirely wrong.
So thoroughly confused or drunk that normal function is impossible.
A person who speaks at length, loudly, and without real content.
Gossip, rumour, and hearsay whispered in hallways.
A face that looks permanently ready to say something unpleasant.
A tiny piece of nonsense that's broken off from a larger piece.
Any task that sounds official but achieves absolutely nothing.
A snort and a chuckle combined into one undignified sound.
To walk in a bumbling, stumbling manner with zero urgency.
More slippery and smooth-talking than is strictly reasonable.
Meaningless fussing over the tiniest of unimportant details.
The odd noise a badly tuned instrument makes at a school concert.
To crumple someone's carefully arranged things without noticing.
A petty, trivial argument that solves absolutely nothing.
The sound a person makes when annoyed and asleep simultaneously.
A small, useless stick found at the bottom of every bag.
A plant that droops dramatically despite being perfectly healthy.
So surprised your mouth falls open and stays there.
Fast, enthusiastic speech that contains no recognisable information.
A tiny decorative object with no purpose except to collect dust.
A person who wanders and wastes time with impressive consistency.
A toothless old person who mutters and chews simultaneously.
A person whose head appears to contain only soft, floppy thoughts.
A word used for any object you cannot or will not name properly.
A person who talks trivialities with enormous enthusiasm.
Looking puzzled and faintly amused at the same time.
A very untidy pile of things that used to be organised.
A laugh that accidentally comes out as a snort.
To idly twist or turn something small between your fingers.
Behaving in a pompous and self-satisfied manner all day.
The strange wistfulness of a second-hand bookshop.
Something that works but nobody can explain how or why.
To gulp something down quickly and greedily.
A small, loud person or animal who won't stop making noise.
Pleasantly plump in a way considered attractive.
To leave somewhere quickly without saying goodbye to anyone.
A verbal argument full of noise and very little logic.
Giving someone an imaginary feast and expecting gratitude.
To drink frequently in small sips, or eat noisily like a duck.
The act of speaking at length about things you barely understand.
A noisy disturbance created by people who refuse to settle down.
To argue noisily about something entirely worthless.
A completely worthless thing kept for no sensible reason.
To laugh very loudly and in an uncontrolled, cackling manner.
An irresistible urge to do something that is clearly unwise.
Reluctant to give information while looking extremely suspicious.
Emotional hardness, or a patch of very tough skin.
A false or unfounded rumour spread with total confidence.
Bad-tempered and bitter in a deeply ingrained way.
A massive semi-aquatic rodent that is bizarrely calm about everything.
Askew, crooked, or positioned in a completely chaotic manner.
A foolish person whose thinking is reliably unreliable.
Using many words to say something that requires far fewer.
To drop things while trying very carefully not to drop things.
A padded pouch once worn on the front of men's trousers. Proudly.
An elderly eccentric, or a bald water bird with a comical beak.
Thoroughly confused to the point of immobility.
Sickness resulting from excessive eating or drinking.
Something full of complicated twists, turns, and unnecessary details.
Irritable and difficult for absolutely no clear reason.
A base, despicable person who probably trips people on staircases.
Cheerfully yellow and slightly wobbling in the breeze.
A brief, casual, and entirely frivolous romantic involvement.
A small, insignificant, or impudent young person.
Wickedly sneaky in a theatrical, cartoonish sort of way.
To take far longer than necessary to do a simple thing.
Expressing absurdity with a completely straight and serious face.
Lying down because you are sick or simply cannot be bothered.
A sudden, rapid decline from success into complete chaos.
Wildly excited or confused, usually both at the same time.
Deliberately being silly for the pure joy of it.
A foolish or inept person, or a stick used to measure oil.
To wander off topic in a conversation and never quite return.
Feeling like the world is spinning, usually after being silly.
To tremble or move weakly, often associated with very old age.
Shaky and slow in movement, especially of elderly people.
A slow, stupid person with an unfortunately confident manner.
Slightly crazy, particularly from boredom or heat.
A silly, gullible person who believes most things they're told.
Slightly eccentric and confused, but in a loveable way.
To let liquid fall from the mouth in an undignified manner.
Nonsensical speech or writing of an embarrassingly low quality.
A person who is thoroughly, spectacularly, and completely boring.
An old English word for a bumblebee. Nothing more, nothing less.
A person considered too dim to learn, usually made to wear a cone.
Irritable and bad-tempered, often from poor digestion.
Irish and Scottish pronunciation of idiot, said with great feeling.
An old-fashioned exclamation of surprise or outrage.
To say something suddenly and exclamatorily. That's all. Stop laughing.
The pleasant plumpness of a well-fed, comfortable person.
To perplex, entangle, and confuse someone completely.
A person who shocks others with unconventional, disruptive behaviour.
A deep, fashionable boredom with the entire concept of things.
Bursting out suddenly, often in an explosive and startling manner.
Pressing your face into your palm in response to absurd behaviour.
A person who faddles about doing nothing important.
To flap about uselessly without achieving any discernible goal.
An elaborate ornament or a showy, useless contrivance.
To tangle something into a complete and confusing mess.
Scottish for showy or fancy in an over-the-top manner.
Wasting time on trivial distractions instead of doing anything useful.
Lacking purpose, direction, or the ability to achieve anything.
Aggressively energetic in a way that surprises people by your size.
Rubbish, nonsense, or the squeezings of whale blubber.
To rummage through something without knowing what you want.
An exclamation of mild annoyance or complete disbelief.
To move about restlessly in a way that irritates everyone nearby.
To obtain something by trickery or clever manipulation.
The mouthpiece of a flute, or any small protruding plug.
To engage in frivolous, playful nonsense with no purpose.
A gadget that spins and whirs, or a silly, flirtatious person.
To astonish someone so completely they momentarily stop existing.
Shockingly wicked, criminal, or just spectacularly bad.
A trick or swindle carried out with charm and confidence.
To keep changing your mind with exhausting frequency.
To fall over heavily, or to spit in a casual, careless manner.
To flood an area with an unreasonable number of opinions.
To make a low, bubbling sound of confusion or dissatisfaction.
Having thoughts that flutter away before they can be caught.
To repeatedly call someone until they give you what you want.
To waste time on something trivially stupid and inconsequential.
A very young, very small fop with enormous self-importance.
A frivolous, silly person, or to waste time on nothing.
Habitually difficult and contrary, refusing all reasonable requests.
Confused and muddled, often from too much thinking or drinking.
A tightrope walker. The word is funnier than it has any right to be.
A steep cable railway. Also the funniest serious word in geography.
An elaborate, unnecessary ruffle or frill on a garment.
Pompous, inflated language that says very little importantly.
A fat, lazy woman, according to people who were very rude in 1700.
To talk rapidly and unintelligibly like an excited goose.
An old man, a boss, or someone who carries a very long stick.
Excessively talkative, especially about trivial matters.
To stare openly and rudely at something or someone.
Dizzy with happiness, excitement, or very fast spinning.
Prone to fits of giggling at entirely inappropriate moments.
Rings for suspending things, or a word that sounds like it should mean something else.
A foolish, stupid person. Short, sharp, and devastating.
The atmospheric period of twilight just after sunset.
Any unpleasant, sticky, shapeless mess of unknown origin.
In a quietly miserable manner, staring at nothing in particular.
Gnarled and twisted, or extremely impressive in a dangerous way.
The mouth, especially when talking too much from it.
Slippery nonsense that gets everywhere and is impossible to clean up.
Something utterly astounding that makes your gob fall open.
Stomach upset caused entirely by nerves and bad decisions.
A silly mistake or a loveable, dopey person.
A person who is endearingly silly in every situation.
Lacking sense and intelligence in a vacant, open-mouthed way.
An Irish word for a young boy with muddy knees and no idea.
An old female cat, or a spiteful, sharp-tongued old woman.
Disgustingly filthy in a way that makes you step back.
To stare at someone eating in hopes of receiving some food.
A small ring of rubber or metal, or a young surfer.
To complain persistently about petty matters. Also a bird.
To complain in a low murmur that's just loud enough to be heard.
Pleased and satisfied. The positive form of disgruntled.
A small, easily caught fish, or a gullible, easily fooled person.
A narrow valley, or the sound of something being swallowed.
Easily deceived and willing to believe almost anything told to them.
The dust, fluff, and mysterious debris at the bottom of a pocket.
Bold initiative and resourcefulness in getting things done.
A dirty, poorly dressed child who lives on the streets.
Looking exhausted and drawn, usually from worrying too much.
A Scottish dish made from things nobody agrees on.
Troubled by anxiety, nightmares, or imaginary problems.
Reckless and badly thought-out, like a hare crossing a motorway.
Having a thin, sharp-featured face of the unfriendly variety.

Mentally dull and lethargic in an almost impressive way.

Bad luck, or a practitioner of a folk magic tradition.

Having been thoroughly cheated or tricked.
American slang for complete and utter nonsense.
A confused mixture of various things thrown together.
Deceptive nonsense, or a hard striped peppermint sweet.

An extreme exaggeration used for emphasis or comic effect.
A person who is deeply and thoroughly ignorant of many things.
A foolish person, technically a historical medical classification.
Disrespectfully bold in a way that leaves adults speechless.
Lacking skill or ability, usually across all relevant areas.
A type of linen tape, or to have a vague, fuzzy suspicion.
Casually unconcerned with things that probably warrant concern.
The punctuation mark ?! used when you're shocked AND asking a question.
Furiously angry in a way that involves a lot of arm movement.

A male donkey, or a person behaving like one.
Invented language or meaningless nonsense, from Lewis Carroll.
A large, joyful, noisy gathering of people for fun.
Using persuasion through endless talking rather than actual authority.
To move quickly and unpredictably to avoid something.
A stupid, blockheaded person with a head full of nonsense.
Alternative spelling of a blockheaded fool. Still accurate.
Given to joking, especially in slightly inappropriate moments.
The process of having a very good time with a lot of noise.
To push and shove someone in a crowd, usually by accident.
A confused mixture of things thrown together without thought.
A variant of kerfuffle. Still chaotic.
A state of depression or unease, or a discordant noise.
To put an end to something with decisive, final force.
A fancy but worthless thing, or an elaborate little dish of food.
A clumsy, hacky solution to a problem that creates more problems.

A baseball pitch that moves unpredictably like a confused bee.
A variant of lollygag. Equally lazy.
To criticise someone harshly and at considerable length.
A sharp satire or ridicule of a person or institution.
Physical or mental weariness that makes effort feel impossible.
A grovelling, servile person who flatters those in power.
Flexible and able to move easily, often embarrassingly so.

Something exceptionally impressive or an outstanding example.
The art and practice of wasting time in a leisurely manner.

Tending to talk a great deal, enthusiastically and at all times.
So absurd as to be amusing in an exasperating way.

Having an extremely unpleasant smell that clears rooms.

Many and various kinds. Also part of a car engine. Useful word.

A deep, rich blue colour, or an old plate used at dinner.
Cheerful, lively enjoyment, often involving laughter and noise.
Slightly offended and quietly annoyed about something minor.

Extremely light drizzle, or to run away without explanation.

Inclined to mope around in a dejected and listless manner.

A pouting expression of displeasure requiring minimal effort.
A state of confusion where nothing quite goes where it should.
A small cake, or a mildly insulting term for a foolish person.

Rubbish, nonsense, or rock from which no ore can be extracted.
A dangerous game involving a knife thrown at the ground near feet.
Confusing, meaningless ritual or language designed to mystify.
Having the mental capacity of a large, passive sheep.

To irritate and annoy someone in a persistent, poking way.

Ungenerous to an extreme and rather embarrassing degree.

Quick and light in movement, often surprisingly so.
A very small amount of liquid, barely worth measuring out.

To make something confusing on purpose to avoid understanding.
Noisy and difficult to control, especially in public places.
An eccentric, strange person who defies normal categorisation.
A term of affection for a person who is being unintentionally silly.

Serving no practical purpose and being quite useless.
The quality of being shocking in a way people secretly enjoy.
Showing excessive confidence in your own opinions and importance.
Scottish word for armpit. Most useful at hugging time.
An important official, or a mock title for a pompous person.

Showy, vain behaviour aimed at attracting admiring attention.
A female peacock who gets none of the credit or plumage.
Pretending to have principles you clearly don't actually possess.
Excessively fussy over minute, unimportant details.
Childishly sulky when you don't get exactly what you want.
Having a slow, calm, unemotional temperament about everything.
A dealer in trivial nonsense who charges full price.
Stubbornly refusing to change your mind regardless of evidence.
The passenger seat on a motorcycle, or any second-rate position.

Mischievous and playful like a small supernatural forest creature.
An archaic word for a bull's organ, later used as a whip.
Lying face down in a random public place. A genuine trend.
Falling or dropping with a soft, wet, satisfying sound.

Smugly satisfied complacency combined with refusal to learn anything.
A tadpole, or a sailor crossing the equator for the first time.
A pompous, self-important person with many grand-sounding titles.
To travel slowly and contentedly with no particular destination.
A vain, conceited person who talks incessantly about themselves.
A darling, or a small loveable person of considerable charm.

A large suitcase, or a word blending parts of two others.
Work produced quickly for money with no artistic ambition.

Using more words than are ever necessary to say a thing.

A term of abuse from the 1700s. Wonderfully specific.

Inclined to pick minor faults with everything around them.

Obstinately defying authority in a stubborn, dug-in manner.
Acting without thought for consequences in an exciting way.
Strongly smelling of something, or strongly suggestive of it.

Deserving mockery and laughter for sheer absurdity.

Round and chubby, tending to roll rather than walk.
A disturbance, quarrel, or noisy disagreement of some scale.
To disturb the smooth surface of something, or someone's calm.
British slang for odd, strange, or rather peculiar.
Boisterous, uncontrollable, and full of rowdy energy.
A person whose presence reliably results in a rumpus.
Having extremely unpleasant manners and habits.
An old insult meaning someone is weak-minded and empty-headed.
Someone who avoids hard work with extreme skill and dedication.
Treating something with contemptuous dismissal and mockery.
To shirk one's duty by inventing implausible excuses.
Making false and damaging claims about someone, outrageously.
Rumour and gossip passed around in hushed, excited tones.
The quality of being pleasantly, harmlessly, and thoroughly silly.
Confused, muddled, or rambling speech of no clear purpose.
The practice of doing everything quickly, badly, and without care.
To perform work in a lazy, careless, and sloppy manner.
A filthy, slobbering rascal of the most unpleasant variety.
Excessively focused on petty, trivial concerns.
Having teeth that stick out at odd and unpredictable angles.
A mythical creature said to seize and devour the unwary.
Sneakily unpleasant and making sly, cutting comments.
A deceptive, dishonest person of the smoothest variety.
An event or person so boring they actively cause unconsciousness.
A breathing tube, or the sound made while half-asleep.
Young, inexperienced, and annoyingly overconfident.
Thoroughly and impressively intoxicated.
A person who splutters indignantly when surprised or offended.
To accidentally swap the initial sounds of two words.
Making explosive, spitting sounds of anger or machinery failure.
Hiding things away compulsively for an emergency that won't come.
Heavy, filling, and very boring. Used for food and for people.
Walking heavily and loudly to express displeasure without words.
The quality of being bad-tempered and uncooperative.
An absolute refusal to change your mind regardless of all evidence.
Inclined to snub others with short, dismissive responses.
A fez-like brimless hat worn across parts of the Middle East.
A ragged, tattered person dressed in shreds and remnants.
To keep changing your position on an issue to avoid commitment.
Any object you know exists but cannot name in the moment.
The quality of being twisted, contrary, and uncooperative.
A state of nervous excitement or anxious confusion.
The quality of acting in a completely silly and playful manner.
Showy but worthless things, or deceitful nonsense.
In a state of extreme dilapidation and about to fall over.
Having the form or shape of a thrush. That's the bird.
A person who trades in meaningless, trivial conversation.
An irritating, insignificant, and rather annoying little person.
Having tightly curled or woolly hair.
Prone to taking offence at the smallest possible imagined slight.
Excessively flattering in a greasy, insincere manner.
To move in a smooth, wave-like, gently rolling motion.
Moving awkwardly and clumsily with no natural grace whatsoever.
Untidy in appearance, as though combs don't exist for you.
Disorderly and difficult to manage in any conventional way.
Having or showing a complete absence of thought or meaning.
In a manner showing excessive pride and boastfulness.
A troublesome wild animal or a mischievous, annoying person.
The quality of using far more words than the situation warrants.
Causing annoyance, frustration, or worry in a persistent way.
A person who designs flags. Yes, it's a real profession.
Wicked and evil in a dramatically theatrical sort of way.
Scottish for exhausted, worn out, or completely depleted.
Fond of making jokes in a playful, witty, slightly roguish way.
To move unsteadily or to feel nauseated and queasy.
Quick to get angry and make biting, sharp remarks.
British term for a stupid or annoying person.
Someone deserving to be hanged, according to old Scottish law.
A person who shifts from opinion to opinion based on the room.
A shoe with an extremely long, pointed toe.
Prone to wobbling in an unstable and unreliable manner.
A carpet shark that sits on the sea floor looking utterly unbothered.
A sturdy, cube-poop-producing Australian marsupial.
Unsteady, crooked, or not working quite as it should.
Feeling dizzy, confused, or slightly unwell.
A bitter plant or the source of painful, bitter feelings.
The act of worrying persistently about things beyond your control.
To flatten or crumple something that was carefully arranged.
To talk noisily and rapidly without conveying anything useful.
To talk or complain continuously and loudly.
An unsophisticated rural person viewed with gentle condescension.
To march over difficult terrain carrying heavy equipment.
Having a full, rounded figure in an attractively soft way.
Absolutely nothing at all. Zero. Nought. Not a thing.
